page.content: The study titled "Ferrari et al." conducted during the period 1983–1986 involved 127 patients (91 male/35 female) with a median age of 15 (range 5–43), classified as Enneking stage IIB, and took place in Austria. "Bacci et al." covered the years 1986–1993 with 29 patients (25 male/4 female), a median age of 46, also in stage IIB, conducted in Italy. The "Edmonson et al." study from 1976–1980 had 38 patients (1 male/37 female), a median age of 17 (range 9–62), classified as stage IIB, in Austria. Research by "Gherlinzoni et al." from 1983–1988 included 355 patients (239 male/116 female) with no specified age or gender data, falling under stage IIA, in Italy. The "Goorin et al." study spanning 1976–1989 reported 74 patients (36 male/38 female) without median age data, classified as stage IIA, also in Austria.

median age of 15 (range 5–43), classified as Enneking stage IIB, and took place in Austria. "Bacci et al." covered the years
1986–1993 with 29 patients (25 male/4 female), a median age of 46, also in stage IIB, conducted in Italy. The "Edmonson et al."
study from 1976–1980 had 38 patients (1 male/37 female), a median age of 17 (range 9–62), classified as stage IIB, in Austria.
Research by "Gherlinzoni et al." from 1983–1988 included 355 patients (239 male/116 female) with no specified age or gender
data, falling under stage IIA, in Italy. The "Goorin et al." study spanning 1976–1989 reported 74 patients (36 male/38 female)
without median age data, classified as stage IIA, also in Austria.

page.content: The "Zhang et al." study from 1981–1997 involved 31 patients (17 male/14 female) with a median age of 15 (range 7–42), in stage IIB, conducted in China. "Sha et al." from 1989–2006 studied 56 patients (35 male/21 female), with a median age of 18 (range 2–46), in stage IIA, in China. "Niu et al." covered 1992–2001 with 189 patients (140 male/49 female) with a median age of 18 (range 4–39), also in stage IIB, in China. The "Bramer et al." study from 1983–2003 comprised 56 patients (44 male/12 female), a median age of 16 (range 3–36), in stage IIB, in America. Lastly, "Ferguson et al." from 1989–2006 involved 31 patients (19 male/12 female) with a median age of 30 (range 11–32), in stage IIB, also in America.
